texto: Matt was the antidote to fear and trepidation. It was as if he’d become so acquainted with death that he could sit in its lap and ask it to tell him bedtime stories.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Gary Ponzo: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Matt was the antidote to fear and trepidation. It was as if he’d become so acquainted with death …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, badass, brave pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e brave.

frase_original: Matt was the antidote to fear and trepidation. It was as if he’d become so acquainted with death that he could sit in its lap and ask it to tell him bedtime stories.

texto: What happened to the perp?” “Perp?” “The piece of crap who tried to kill my wife. Where is he now?” There was a pause, then, “Well, uh, you see, the guy—he’s in the parking lot right now.” “What’s he doing there? Is he being arrested?” “Actually, he’s resting. As a matter of fact, he’s going to be resting for a really long time.” Nick understood the term. “Tommy, by any chance did he stumble upon an open window?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Gary Ponzo que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “What happened to the perp?” “Perp?” “The piece of crap who tried to kill my wife. Where is he now…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sucesso, anger, mobster, com foco em mobster e payback.

frase_original: What happened to the perp?” “Perp?” “The piece of crap who tried to kill my wife. Where is he now?” There was a pause, then, “Well, uh, you see, the guy—he’s in the parking lot right now.” “What’s he doing there? Is he being arrested?” “Actually, he’s resting. As a matter of fact, he’s going to be resting for a really long time.” Nick understood the term. “Tommy, by any chance did he stumble upon an open window?

texto: A vision flashed across Nick’s mind. It was the image of a lipstick kiss his wife left for him on the mirror that morning. It hung there like the single digit sum to the chalkboard-crammed equation of his life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Gary Ponzo: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “A vision flashed across Nick’s mind. It was the image of a lipstick kiss his wife left for him on…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, last-moment, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e love.

frase_original: A vision flashed across Nick’s mind. It was the image of a lipstick kiss his wife left for him on the mirror that morning. It hung there like the single digit sum to the chalkboard-crammed equation of his life.
